Books Printed, and sold by Charls Tyus at the three Bibles, on London-Bridge.

List View

  • A Learned Expoſition on the X Commandments, by Mr. Dod in 4.

    * Tyus ed. not in ESTC

  • Susurrium cum Deo, or holy Soliloquies of the Devout soul, by J.H. late Bishop of Norwich.

    * 1659. Susurrium cum Deo. Soliloquies: or, Holy selfe-conferences of the devout soul, upon sundry choice occasions; with humble addresses to the throne of grace. Together with the souls farwel to earth, and approaches to heaven. By Jos. Hall, B. Norwich. The third edition. London : printed by W. H. [William Hunt] for Charles Tyus at the sign of the three Bibles on the middle of London-Bridge. 12⁰: [10], 288, 287-391, [3] p. ESTC R202339; Wing H421A

  • Comfort and Counsel for Dejected souls, by J. Durant. in 8.

    * 1658. Comfort and counsell for dejected soules. Or A treatise concerning spirituall dejection. In which is handled, 1 the nature 2 the working 3 the grounds 4 the remedies of spiritual dejection. And in which is held forth, satisfaction to some particular cases, and general advice for any soul who is cast down. Being the heads and sum of divers sermons preached to a particular congregation, from Psalm 42. last. By John Durant, preacher of the Gospel, and pastor of a Church of Christ in Canterbury. London : printed for R.I. and are to be sold by Charles Tyus at the Three Cups on London Bridge. 8⁰: [16], 259, [11] p. ESTC R208872; Wing D2675

  • Sips of sweetnesse: or, Consolation for weak Beleevers, by J. D. in 8.

    * Tyus ed. not in ESTC

  • The Pilgrims Port: or, The weary mans rest in the Grave, by Mr. G. Eubank.

    * 166[0]. The pilgrims port or The weary mans rest in the grave opened and improved in a sermon, at the funeral of the honorable Ms. Margaret Marwood, wife to Hen Marwood Esq; together with a character of the deceased gentlewoman; briefly describing the tenure of her life, and manner of her death. London : printed for Charls Tyus at the Three Bibles on London-Bridge. [sic] and for R. Lambert in the Minsteryard in York. 12⁰: [24], 96, 73-184, [6] p. ESTC R176803; Wing E3555C

  • The Womans labours: or, the worthy works of a weaker Vessel, being Poetical Verses on several places of Scripture.

    * Not in ESTC

  • The famous History of Palmerin of England, in two parts, in 4.

    * 1664: ESTC R201969; Wing M2613B

  • The Antient History of the Destruction of Troy, in three parts. in 4.

    * 1663: ESTC R22835; Wing L929

  • The Famous and Delightful History of Valentine and Orson, the two Sons of the Emperour of Greece.

    *

  • The Honour of Chivalry, Or the Renowned History of Don Belliante of Greece.

    *

  • The pleasant History of Fragosa and his three Sons.

    *

  • There is also newly Printed a very Pleasant, and Delightful History, of Palladine of England.

    *

  • The pleasant History of Cleocreton, and Cloriana, a new History, very delightful to read

    *

  • The Book of Palmestry, in 8.

    *

  • There is also lately Printed, a Book of Knowledge in twelve Sheets, teaching the Art of Astrologie, with Arithmatick, and a variety of other new inventions, the like not extant. There is also, a new Wheel of Fortune, resolving several questions, very easie to bee understood, and delightful to the Reader.

    *

  • The Pleasant History of Jack of Newberry.

    *

  • The Play of Fryer Bacon, in 4.

    *

  • Scarborow Spaw, Or, a description of the Nature, and Vertue, of the water of the Spaw in York-shire.

    *

  • Conceited Letters, in 4.

    *

  • The Book of Cookery, teaching the good Houswife how to order all manner of Flesh and Fish, in 8.

    *

  • The Garland of Loyalty, containing most of the Loyal songs of these late times.

    *

  • The Jovial Garland, containing all new merry Songs.

    *

  • The pleasant History of Parismus in three sheets.

    *


[Bound with Peter Levens, The path-way to health, 1664.]
